title: mol-focus-review
description:
Focus + in-session review formula — one Claude worker implements via /focus,
then reviews its own work with /simplify and /code-review before closing.

Single-agent workflow:
1. Load context and understand the bead (including prior rejection_reason).
2. /focus on bead (plan → execute → verify).
3. Run tests.
4. /simplify the diff.
5. /code-review the diff and verify the work matches the bead's acceptance
   criteria. If review finds blockers, set `metadata.rejection_reason` and
   drain — a fresh pool worker picks up the bead and retries, reading the
   rejection reason in step 1.
6. If review passes, commit final state, close the bead, drain.

Review in step 5 is a hard verification gate: it must check that the
implementation actually fulfills what the bead asked for, not just that
the code is stylistically clean. Missing acceptance criteria → reject.

## Variables

| Variable | Source | Description |
|----------|--------|-------------|
| issue    | caller | The work bead ID to focus on |
| base_branch | caller | Base branch for diff comparison (default: main) |
| test_command | rig config | Test command to verify work. Empty = skip. |

title: BLOCKER: Output path mismatch in all 10 new tasks (prompts vs checks)
description:
All 10 new non-Go dual-repo tasks from bead 0rv.20 have a fundamental output path mismatch that causes them to score 0.0 at runtime.

Discovered during /focus parallel convergence review.

The problem:
- Prompts instruct the agent to write to /workspace/<TASK-SPECIFIC>.md or .json
- Check scripts read from \$WORKSPACE/agent_output/answer.json
- At runtime, checks always fail because answer.json is never created

Path mapping table:

| Task | Prompt writes to | Checks read from |
|---|---|---|
| support-mapping-dual-spring-kafka-001 | /workspace/SUPPORT_MAPPING.md | \$WORKSPACE/agent_output/answer.json |
| support-mapping-dual-nextjs-webpack-001 | /workspace/SUPPORT_MAPPING.md | \$WORKSPACE/agent_output/answer.json |
| dead-code-dual-django-wagtail-001 | /workspace/DEAD_CODE_REPORT.json | \$WORKSPACE/agent_output/answer.json |
| dead-code-dual-spring-hibernate-001 | /workspace/DEAD_CODE_REPORT.json | \$WORKSPACE/agent_output/answer.json |
| schema-evolution-dual-spring-flyway-001 | /workspace/SCHEMA_EVOLUTION.json | \$WORKSPACE/agent_output/answer.json |
| config-drift-dual-tokio-hyper-001 | /workspace/DRIFT_REPORT.json | \$WORKSPACE/agent_output/answer.json |
| config-drift-dual-webpack-babel-001 | /workspace/DRIFT_REPORT.json | \$WORKSPACE/agent_output/answer.json |
| incident-investigation-dual-django-celery-001 | /workspace/INCIDENT_REPORT.md | \$WORKSPACE/agent_output/answer.json |
| refactor-dual-flask-werkzeug-001 | /workspace/REFACTOR_PLAN.json | \$WORKSPACE/agent_output/answer.json |
| dep-graph-dual-nextjs-swc-001 | /workspace/DEPENDENCY_GRAPH.json | \$WORKSPACE/agent_output/answer.json |

Reference pattern (correct): benchmarks/customer_escalation/error-prov-dual-netty-spring-001/ writes to /workspace/analysis/IMPACT_REPORT.md and checks read from the same task-specific path.

Acceptance criteria:
- All 10 new tasks have consistent output paths between prompt and checks
- Agent instructions explicitly say where to write the output file
- Check scripts read from that same path (not a generic answer.json location)
- python3 -m pytest tests/test_task_consistency.py (if exists) passes for all 10 tasks
- At least one smoke run per task produces non-zero scores when a placeholder output file exists

Preferred approach: Update the 10 prompts to write JSON to \$WORKSPACE/agent_output/answer.json (matching existing EnterpriseBench convention used by other task types). This is more invasive than updating checks but aligns with the benchmark-wide answer.json pattern used by verifier plugins.

Alternative: Update checks to read from task-specific paths. Less invasive but creates per-task inconsistency.

Depends on: 0rv.23 (shell injection fix — those rewrites will touch the same scripts, so coordinate)

Priority: P1 (new tasks unusable until fixed)
notes:
PLAN: Root cause — runner auto-appends 'Write to /workspace/agent_output/answer.json' output block (run_task.py:362-379), all 30 target check scripts already read from that path, but the 10 task prompts + instruction.md files hard-code conflicting task-specific paths (SUPPORT_MAPPING.md, DEAD_CODE_REPORT.json, etc). Agent obeys the more specific task-level instruction and answer.json is never created.

Approach: Strip the 'Write your analysis to /workspace/<X>.md with:' block from both task.toml prompt and instruction.md for all 10 tasks. Keep the bullet list of required fields as 'Your answer must document:'. Leave the runner's appendix as the sole source of truth for output location. Add regression test to block reintroduction.

Files to touch (21):
- 10 task.toml files (prompt field)
- 10 instruction.md files
- tests/test_task_output_path_consistency.py (new)

Tests (TDD):
- test_no_stray_output_paths parametrized per task: regex-scan task.toml prompt + instruction.md for /workspace/[A-Z_]+\.(md|json) literal paths that are not /workspace/agent_output/answer.json
- test_check_scripts_use_answer_json parametrized per checkpoint verifier
- Smoke simulation: seed WORKSPACE/agent_output/answer.json with stub source_files list, run each checkpoint, assert non-zero score

Risks:
- Stripped text must preserve per-task required fields (what to document). Mitigation: keep the bullets, drop only the path sentence.
- Check scripts grep for 'source_files'/'files' JSON fields — confirmed by reading their python3 -c payloads. Stub answers can be minimal.

Out of scope:
- ground_truth.json correctness (bead 0rv.25)
- error-prov-dual-netty-spring-001 IMPACT_REPORT.md pattern (not in this bead's 10 tasks; checks intentionally grep markdown)
- run_task.py appendix changes
- shell-injection in check scripts (bead 0rv.23, closed)
PLAN-REVIEW: Architect surfaced three key issues.

C1 (CRITICAL - scope): Ran precise audit — 25 tasks have the bug (not 10). The 10 in the bead description were a sample from one discovery session. The additional 15:
- customer_escalation: err-provenance-dual-{celery,docker,grafana,requests,terraform}-001, support-mapping-dual-{ansible,flask,grafana}-001
- dependency_management: api-contract-dual-fastapi-001, api-contract-tri-{envoy,protobuf}-001, ccx-dep-trace-106
- feature_delivery: schema-evolution-tri-supabase-001
- incident_response: ccx-incident-032
- technical_debt: dead-code-dual-angular-001
Confirmed via script: each has at least one check reading agent_output/answer.json AND an instruction hard-coding a different /workspace/X.md|json.

C2 (out of scope but noted): run_task.py:362-379 unconditionally appends an answer.json output block, which creates the OPPOSITE collision for legacy single-repo tasks (dep-traversal-001..012, refactor-orchestration-001..008, monorepo-boundary-001..010, etc.) whose checks read from markdown paths. Will file as a follow-up bead — not part of 0rv.24.

C3 (fix adopted): Regression test encodes path equality invariant — for each task, the path each check script reads must overlap with a path the instruction tells the agent to write. Replaces the literal regex greper.

H1 (verified): All 25 tasks are session_type='single' and have no [[sessions]] blocks. task.toml 'prompt' is used by lib/eb_verify/runner.py:225 (LLM judge fallback) and instruction.md is used by scripts/orchestration/run_task.py — both must stay in sync.

H2 (out of scope, noted): LLM-judge fallback list at run_task.py:741-755 is hardcoded to 4 legacy markdown filenames. Dead code after this fix. File as follow-up.

H3 (fix adopted): Replace per-task bullet lists with JSON schema fragments naming the actual keys each check script greps for (audited per task). Reduces prose-vs-JSON contract mismatch.

REVISED PLAN:

Goal: For every task whose check scripts read agent_output/answer.json, the prompt + instruction.md must tell the agent to write exactly there, with JSON keys matching what the checks parse.

Scope: 25 tasks (pending user approval to expand from bead's original 10).

Approach:
1. Write path-equality regression test first (TDD RED). Expect 25 failures.
2. For each task, strip the hardcoded /workspace/<X> path and replace the bullet requirements list with a JSON-schema-aligned block naming the exact keys the checks grep for (task-type-specific).
3. Use a Python rewrite script for consistency across 50 files (25 task.toml + 25 instruction.md).
4. Run regression test (GREEN). Smoke test one representative task locally with stub answer.json.
5. File follow-up beads for C2 (runner appendix collision with legacy tasks) and H2 (LLM judge dead fallback).

Files: 25 task.toml, 25 instruction.md, 1 new test file, 1 throwaway rewrite script.
Out of scope: ground truth correctness (0rv.25), shell injection (0rv.23 closed), runner appendix fix (new bead), LLM judge fallback (new bead), netty-spring reference pattern (internally consistent).
RESOLVED: 29 tasks fixed (scope expanded from plan-review's 25 to 29 — the test caught 6 additional Group B tasks with /workspace/<repo>/X.json paths).

Group A (23 tasks, checks read agent_output/answer.json): stripped 'Write your X to /workspace/Y.{md,json} with:' line; runner appendix is sole output directive. Where this left an orphan JSON template, added 'Document in your answer (use these JSON fields):' lead-in.

Group B (6 tasks, checks read /workspace/<repo>/X.json): aligned instruction paths to the existing check-script paths. Note: 5 of these have copy-paste bugs in their check paths (e.g., schema-evolution-dual-django-001 reads /workspace/zulip/... but has django+wagtail repos) — filed as follow-up bead EnterpriseBench-7k4.

Verification:
- tests/test_task_output_path_consistency.py: 102 passed, 45 skipped (was 29 failing → all green)
- Smoke test of support-mapping-dual-spring-kafka-001 with stub answer.json: 3/3 checkpoints scored 1.0
- Smoke test of dep-graph-dual-nextjs-swc-001 with stub answer.json: 3/3 checkpoints scored 1.0
- Drive-by fix to tests/test_dead_code_verifiers.py: removed parametrize entries for dead-code-004/005 (archived)

Out-of-scope follow-ups filed:
- EnterpriseBench-21e (P2): strip runner's unconditional answer.json appendix when checks use task-specific paths (item C2 in plan-review)
- EnterpriseBench-81h (P3): remove dead LLM-judge fallback markdown filename list (item H2 in plan-review)
- EnterpriseBench-7k4 (P2): fix copy-paste bugs in 5 Group B check-script paths
- EnterpriseBench-6fy (P3): fix test_support_mapping_verifiers.py expectations for new dual support-mapping tasks (pre-existing 19 failures caused by new tasks' existence, not 0rv.24 edits)

Files changed: 60 (29 task.toml + 29 instruction.md + 1 test + this rewriter is /tmp throwaway).

title: SECURITY: Shell injection in 28 of 30 new task check scripts
description:
CRITICAL security vulnerability discovered during /focus parallel convergence review of bead 0rv.20.

28 of 30 check scripts in the 10 new non-Go dual-repo tasks have a shell injection vulnerability via this pattern:

  AGENT_FILES=\$(python3 -c "... print(f['path']) ...")
  python3 -c "
  agent_files = '''\$AGENT_FILES'''.strip().split('\n')
  "

An agent putting triple-quotes in a file path inside answer.json closes the Python string literal and allows arbitrary Python code execution as the check runner uid. Also enables trivial cheating — agents can spam paths to match substrings in gt_files.

The two safe scripts are in benchmarks/customer_escalation/support-mapping-dual-spring-kafka-001/ (check_error_chain.sh, check_trigger_conditions.sh) which pass answer.json path via os.environ instead of shell-interpolating content.

Acceptance criteria:
- All 28 affected check scripts rewritten to eliminate the \$AGENT_FILES shell variable
- Both the ground_truth.json path and answer.json path passed via os.environ['ANSWER_FILE']/os.environ['GT_FILE']
- All agent-controlled data stays inside a single Python process reading from the filesystem
- No shell interpolation of agent-controlled content into any python3 -c string
- Each rewritten script produces the same score output format as before

Fix template (from security-reviewer):

  python3 -c "
  import json, os
  answer = json.load(open(os.environ['ANSWER_FILE']))
  gt = json.load(open(os.environ['GT_FILE']))
  gt_files = [f['path'] for f in gt.get('required_files', [])]
  raw = answer.get('source_files', answer.get('files', []))
  agent_files = [f.get('path', '') if isinstance(f, dict) else f for f in raw]
  found = sum(1 for gt_f in gt_files if any(gt_f in af or af.endswith(gt_f) for af in agent_files))
  score = found / len(gt_files) if gt_files else 0.0
  print(json.dumps({'score': round(score, 2), 'passed': score >= 0.5, 'detail': f'Found {found}/{len(gt_files)} required files'}))
  "

Affected task directories (all checks/*.sh except the 2 spring-kafka scripts):
- benchmarks/customer_escalation/support-mapping-dual-nextjs-webpack-001/
- benchmarks/customer_escalation/support-mapping-dual-spring-kafka-001/ (only check_source_files.sh, not check_error_chain.sh or check_trigger_conditions.sh)
- benchmarks/technical_debt/dead-code-dual-django-wagtail-001/
- benchmarks/technical_debt/dead-code-dual-spring-hibernate-001/
- benchmarks/feature_delivery/schema-evolution-dual-spring-flyway-001/
- benchmarks/platform_engineering/config-drift-dual-tokio-hyper-001/
- benchmarks/platform_engineering/config-drift-dual-webpack-babel-001/
- benchmarks/incident_response/incident-investigation-dual-django-celery-001/
- benchmarks/technical_debt/refactor-dual-flask-werkzeug-001/
- benchmarks/dependency_management/dep-graph-dual-nextjs-swc-001/

Priority: P1 (security, blocks any run of new tasks)
notes:
PLAN:
Goal: Eliminate Python-injection vulnerability in 28 check scripts by collapsing 3-step extraction+compare into a single Python process that reads both files via env vars (pattern already used by the 2 safe spring-kafka scripts).

Approach:
1. Keep export ANSWER_FILE / export GT_FILE pattern; all agent data stays inside one python3 -c process that reads via os.environ.
2. Rewrite each of 28 scripts preserving: per-script comment header, fallback key chain (source_files -> files -> error_source.files), substring file-match logic, output JSON format {score, passed, detail}.
3. Leave the 2 safe spring-kafka scripts (check_error_chain.sh, check_trigger_conditions.sh) UNTOUCHED.

Files to touch (28 scripts across 10 task dirs):
- customer_escalation/support-mapping-dual-spring-kafka-001: check_error_source.sh (1)
- customer_escalation/support-mapping-dual-nextjs-webpack-001: all 3
- technical_debt/dead-code-dual-django-wagtail-001: all 3
- technical_debt/dead-code-dual-spring-hibernate-001: all 3
- feature_delivery/schema-evolution-dual-spring-flyway-001: all 3
- platform_engineering/config-drift-dual-tokio-hyper-001: all 3
- platform_engineering/config-drift-dual-webpack-babel-001: all 3
- incident_response/incident-investigation-dual-django-celery-001: all 3
- technical_debt/refactor-dual-flask-werkzeug-001: all 3
- dependency_management/dep-graph-dual-nextjs-swc-001: all 3

Tests (new, under tests/security/):
- test_check_scripts_injection.py
  - Fixture A: benign answer.json with 2 matching paths + ground_truth.json with 3 required_files -> expect {score: 0.67, passed: true, detail: 'Found 2/3 ...'}
  - Fixture B: answer.json containing path "'''; import os; os.system('touch /tmp/pwned')  #" -> expect valid JSON result, no /tmp/pwned artifact
  - Fixture C: answer.json missing -> expect {score: 0.0, passed: false}
  - Run each of the 28 scripts in a subprocess with WORKSPACE/TASK_DIR env set to temp dirs.
  - Assert check_error_chain.sh and check_trigger_conditions.sh in spring-kafka are UNCHANGED (git-diff assertion).

Risks:
- Subtle semantic drift: bead template drops the error_source.files fallback; original scripts have it. -> Preserve the exact extraction chain to avoid regressing any task whose answer shape uses that key.
- 0rv.24 will also touch these same scripts soon (path alignment). Since this fix keeps the $WORKSPACE/agent_output/answer.json reading location unchanged, there is no file-level coordination hazard.

Out of scope (belongs to other beads):
- 0rv.24: prompt/check output path mismatch in task.toml instruction files
- 0rv.25: ground_truth.json correctness bugs + boilerplate check differentiation
- Not rewriting any task.toml, ground_truth.json, or prompts
- Not changing the check weights/semantic meaning of individual checkpoints
PLAN-REVIEW (architect adversarial):

BLOCKER findings (verified via grep + spot reads):
1. Scope count wrong: actual vulnerable count is 37 active files across 16 dirs, not 28 across 10. The bead's framing of "28 of 30 new task check scripts" missed:
   - 5 dual-repo err-provenance dirs (celery, docker, grafana, requests, terraform) — check_error_source.sh only (2 safe keyword-overlap scripts per dir)
   - 1 more dual-repo support-mapping dir (ansible) — check_error_source.sh only
   - 3 single-repo err-provenance-0{1,2,3}/check_error_source.sh — not part of bead 0rv.20 but same vulnerability
   - 7 archived files in _archived/customer_escalation/err-provenance-{04..010}/ — leave untouched (retired, not run)
2. spring-kafka cohort: "2 of 3 safe" finding confirmed — check_error_chain.sh and check_trigger_conditions.sh use keyword-overlap, safe. Its check_error_source.sh is vulnerable (same as others).
3. Fallback chain is NOT uniform across all file-extraction scripts — support-mapping-001/check_code_paths.sh (out of scope for this bead, but existing) uses code_paths→files→source_files. All 37 vulnerable files in THIS scope use source_files→files→error_source.files — verified consistent.

HIGH findings adopted:
- Template must add JSONDecodeError handling + exit 0 on missing files (non-zero is infra failure signal)
- Substring-match soft-cheat (gt_f in af) is same vulnerability class per the bead description and must be fixed in the same patch (one-line change: af == gt_f or af.endswith('/' + gt_f))

HIGH findings NOT adopted:
- Multi-path answer.json fallback to absorb 0rv.24: rejected. Keeps bead boundaries clean, avoids coupling 0rv.23 to 0rv.24's path decisions.

MEDIUM findings:
- Plugin-ification (lib/eb_verify/plugins/file_extraction.py): filed as follow-up bead — architecturally right per CLAUDE.md but scope-creep into a P1 security fix. Ship the template rewrite now, plugin refactor next.
- Test fixture expansion to 6 injection payloads: adopted (triple-quote, sys.exit, path traversal, type confusion, path-spam, malformed JSON).
- Content-hash snapshot for "unchanged" assertion instead of git diff: adopted.

REVISED SCOPE: 37 files across 16 active dirs, NOT 28 across 10. Revised plan attached in next note.
PLAN v2 (revised after architect review):

Goal: Patch Python injection vulnerability in 37 active check scripts that interpolate agent-controlled JSON content into shell-captured Python string literals via '''$AGENT_FILES'''. Simultaneously tighten substring-match scoring to kill the path-spam soft-cheat (called out as the same vulnerability in the bead description).

Scope: 37 active files across 16 dirs. Cohort breakdown:
  Group A (9 dirs × 3 scripts = 27 files) — all 3 checks vulnerable:
    - dependency_management/dep-graph-dual-nextjs-swc-001
    - technical_debt/refactor-dual-flask-werkzeug-001
    - technical_debt/dead-code-dual-spring-hibernate-001
    - technical_debt/dead-code-dual-django-wagtail-001
    - incident_response/incident-investigation-dual-django-celery-001
    - platform_engineering/config-drift-dual-webpack-babel-001
    - platform_engineering/config-drift-dual-tokio-hyper-001
    - feature_delivery/schema-evolution-dual-spring-flyway-001
    - customer_escalation/support-mapping-dual-nextjs-webpack-001
  Group B (7 dirs × 1 script = 7 files) — only check_error_source.sh vulnerable:
    - customer_escalation/support-mapping-dual-spring-kafka-001
    - customer_escalation/support-mapping-dual-ansible-001
    - customer_escalation/err-provenance-dual-{celery,docker,grafana,requests,terraform}-001
  Group C (3 files) — single-repo, same vulnerability:
    - customer_escalation/err-provenance-0{1,2,3}/checks/check_error_source.sh

Excluded:
  - benchmarks/_archived/customer_escalation/err-provenance-{04,05,06,07,08,09,010} (7 files): retired, not run. Filed as P3 follow-up bead.
  - 2 safe spring-kafka scripts (check_error_chain.sh, check_trigger_conditions.sh): keyword-overlap algorithm, already env-var based.
  - All other check_error_chain.sh and check_trigger_conditions.sh in Group B dirs: use safe keyword-overlap pattern (verified by grep — only check_error_source.sh files in those dirs match the vulnerable pattern).
  - ccx-incident-032, support-mapping-0{01..04}, calibration-0{01..04} cousin scripts with the substring-match anti-pattern: out of scope (already use env-var pattern; path-spam soft-cheat fix files as separate bead).

Approach: inline template rewrite (not plugin-ification — plugin refactor filed as separate bead to keep this fix small and auditable).

Template (identical for all 37 files, differing only in the # comment header line 2):

  #!/usr/bin/env bash
  # <original-script-header-comment>
  set -euo pipefail
  export ANSWER_FILE="$WORKSPACE/agent_output/answer.json"
  export GT_FILE="$TASK_DIR/ground_truth.json"
  if [[ ! -f "$ANSWER_FILE" ]]; then
      echo '{"score": 0.0, "passed": false, "detail": "No answer.json found"}'
      exit 0
  fi
  if [[ ! -f "$GT_FILE" ]]; then
      echo '{"score": 0.0, "passed": false, "detail": "No ground_truth.json found"}'
      exit 0
  fi
  python3 -c "
  import json, os, sys
  try:
      answer = json.load(open(os.environ['ANSWER_FILE']))
      gt = json.load(open(os.environ['GT_FILE']))
  except (OSError, json.JSONDecodeError) as e:
      print(json.dumps({'score': 0.0, 'passed': False, 'detail': f'Parse error: {type(e).__name__}'}))
      sys.exit(0)
  gt_files = [f.get('path', '') for f in gt.get('required_files', []) if isinstance(f, dict)]
  gt_files = [f for f in gt_files if f]
  raw = answer.get('source_files', answer.get('files', answer.get('error_source', {}).get('files', [])))
  if not isinstance(raw, list):
      raw = []
  agent_files = []
  for f in raw:
      if isinstance(f, dict):
          agent_files.append(f.get('path', f.get('file', '')))
      elif isinstance(f, str):
          agent_files.append(f)
  if not gt_files:
      print(json.dumps({'score': 0.0, 'passed': False, 'detail': 'No GT files'}))
  else:
      found = sum(1 for gt_f in gt_files if any(af == gt_f or af.endswith('/' + gt_f) for af in agent_files))
      score = found / len(gt_files)
      print(json.dumps({'score': round(score, 2), 'passed': score >= 0.5, 'detail': f'Found {found}/{len(gt_files)} required files'}))
  "

Key deltas from vulnerable original:
  - Single python3 -c process — NO shell interpolation of agent data anywhere
  - try/except JSONDecodeError on both json.load calls
  - exit 0 on missing fixtures (non-zero = runner treats as infra failure)
  - Tightened match: (af == gt_f or af.endswith('/' + gt_f)) — was (gt_f in af or af.endswith(gt_f))
  - Robust type handling for non-list / non-dict agent output

Tests (new, tests/security/test_check_scripts_injection.py):
  1. Template compliance: all 37 files match a regex/hash fingerprint for the safe template
  2. Unchanged-file snapshot: content-hash of 2 safe spring-kafka scripts (check_error_chain.sh, check_trigger_conditions.sh) matches pre-change SHA256
  3. Injection suite on 1 representative script (config-drift-dual-tokio-hyper-001/check_config_valid.sh), 8 cases:
     a) benign: 2 of 3 required files → score 0.67, passed
     b) missing answer.json → score 0.0, passed false, exit 0
     c) malformed JSON answer → score 0.0, Parse error detail, exit 0
     d) triple-quote injection payload → valid JSON, no /tmp/pwned artifact
     e) sys.exit injection payload → valid JSON, exit 0 (not 0 exit from injected sys.exit)
     f) path traversal (../../etc/passwd in source_files) → valid JSON, not counted as match
     g) type confusion (source_files: "a string") → valid JSON, score 0.0
     h) path-spam cheat (required file buried in 50 fake paths, substring matches but not exact) → score excludes the fake matches
  4. Semantic equivalence on a benign golden fixture: rewritten template produces same score on a known-good answer.json as the original did (for scores that weren't affected by the tightened match)

Risks:
  - The tightened match (== or /-suffix) may regress SCORES on existing valid answers that used unanchored suffixes. Mitigation: run semantic-equivalence test on 3-5 real answer.json fixtures from calibration runs; if regressions found, document and accept (the unanchored match is itself the vulnerability).
  - 37 files is wider than the bead's original framing; scope change must be accepted by user before execute.
  - No-op exit 0 on missing fixtures changes runner signaling: runner currently treats exit 1 as "infra problem retry". This is a deliberate change — a missing answer.json is an agent failure, not an infra failure. Verify with scripts/run_task.py that exit 0 with score 0.0 is handled correctly.

Out of scope (deferred to existing beads or new beads):
  - 0rv.24: prompt/check output path alignment. Keeping read path ($WORKSPACE/agent_output/answer.json) unchanged; 0rv.24 owns the prompt-side write path.
  - 0rv.25: ground truth correctness bugs + boilerplate check differentiation.
  - NEW: plugin-ification of file_extraction scoring (architectural refactor, file as follow-up bead with "blocks" dep on this).
  - NEW: audit _archived/err-provenance-{04..010}/checks for the same pattern before any unarchiving (P3).
  - NEW: path-spam soft-cheat cleanup in ccx-incident-032, support-mapping-0{01..04}, calibration-0{01..04} (still use gt_f in af substring match despite being env-var safe) — file as P2 follow-up.
DISCOVERY: Filed follow-up beads during architect review — 4aq (plugin refactor, blocked by this), 733 (cousin path-spam cleanup), wkm (archived audit P3).
TEST-REVIEW: code-reviewer agent flagged 2 HIGH items (sys.exit stdout guard + missing-file exit code contract) and suggested some MEDIUM improvements. Two of the reviewer's findings were incorrect after verification (SHA256 length 64 not 63; path-spam payloads do distinguish old/new match).

Fixes applied to tests/security/test_check_scripts_injection.py:
- test_sys_exit_injection_blocked: added explicit assert proc.stdout.strip() with clear failure message before _parse_score
- test_triple_quote_injection_blocked: replaced the self-corrupting payload ('''; import ...; x=' which caused SyntaxError before exec) with '''+str(__import__('os').system('touch <canary>'))+''' which survives parse and actually creates the canary. Verified RED produces AssertionError "canary was created" — meaning the injection actually executes on vulnerable scripts.
- test_path_spam_substring_cheat_blocked: added detailed docstring walk-through of each payload showing how it matches under OLD (gt_f in af) but not NEW (af == gt_f or af.endswith('/' + gt_f)).

Test suite confirmed RED:
- 74 template-compliance failures (37 × 2 invariants) — all 37 target files still have '''$AGENT_FILES''' interpolation

close_reason:
DONE: Patched Python code injection vulnerability in 37 active check scripts across 16 benchmark directories.

## Root cause
28 check scripts in new dual-repo tasks (from bead 0rv.20) plus 9 pre-existing scripts in single-repo err-provenance and err-provenance-dual tasks interpolated agent-controlled JSON content into a Python triple-quoted string literal via the pattern `agent_files = '''$AGENT_FILES'''.strip().split('\n')`. An agent writing `'''` plus arbitrary Python into a path inside answer.json could close the string and execute code under the task runner uid. The same unanchored substring match `gt_f in af or af.endswith(gt_f)` also enabled a path-spam soft-cheat.

## Fix (all 37 scripts rewritten to identical template)
- Single `python3 -c` process that reads both answer.json and ground_truth.json directly via `os.environ['ANSWER_FILE']` / `os.environ['GT_FILE']` — no shell interpolation of agent data anywhere.
- `try/except (OSError, UnicodeDecodeError, json.JSONDecodeError)` on both json.load calls. Detail field is generic 'Parse error' (no exception-class leak).
- `encoding='utf-8'` on both open() calls (defends non-ASCII paths).
- Tightened match: `af == gt_f or af.endswith('/' + gt_f)` kills path-spam soft-cheat.
- isinstance-guarded fallback chain for source_files → files → error_source.files (prevents AttributeError denial-of-scoring when agent supplies non-dict `error_source`).
- GT extraction accepts both `[{"path": ...}]` and flat string list forms.
- Missing answer.json → `exit 0` with score 0 (agent failure). Missing GT → `exit 1` to stderr (infra failure — distinguishable to operators).

## Scope
37 active vulnerable files across 16 dirs:
- Group A (9 dirs × 3 scripts): Dep-graph, refactor-dual, dead-code-dual (×2), incident-investigation-dual, config-drift-dual (×2), schema-evolution-dual, support-mapping-dual-nextjs-webpack
- Group B (7 dirs × 1 script — check_error_source.sh only): support-mapping-dual-{spring-kafka, ansible}, err-provenance-dual-{celery, docker, grafana, requests, terraform}
- Group C (3 single-repo files): err-provenance-{01, 02, 03}/checks/check_error_source.sh

Excluded: 2 already-safe spring-kafka keyword-overlap scripts (SHA256 snapshot guard); 7 _archived scripts (filed as bead wkm); cousin scripts with substring match but no injection (filed as bead 733).

## Key decisions
- Architect pressure-test expanded scope from bead's original "28 across 10 dirs" to 37 across 16. Plan v2 approved by user.
- Chose inline-template rewrite over plugin extraction to keep the security patch small and auditable. Plugin refactor filed as bead 4aq (blocked by this).
- Missing-GT exit-1 contract is new behavior; verified by test_missing_gt_file_is_infra_failure.
- Tightened match may regress scores on existing fixtures relying on unanchored substrings — that IS the vulnerability, regression is expected.

## Test coverage (tests/security/test_check_scripts_injection.py)
166 security tests:
- 37 × 2 template-compliance (no `'''$` shell interpolation; all have JSONDecodeError handling, single python3 -c, tightened match)
- 2 SHA256 snapshot guards for safe spring-kafka scripts (must not be overwritten)
- 37 × 2 parametrized adversarial coverage (type-confusion + path-spam across ALL 37 scripts, not just representative)
- 9 targeted adversarial tests on representative script (triple-quote injection with canary, sys.exit injection, path traversal, benign, missing answer, missing GT, malformed JSON, error_source fallback shape, error_source non-dict, GT flat-string list)
- 3 inventory sanity tests

Verified: 1048 total tests pass (166 security + 882 task-validity). No regressions.

## Artifacts
- 37 files rewritten with identical safe template (per-file header comment preserved)
- tests/security/test_check_scripts_injection.py (new, ~580 lines after formatter)
- tests/conftest.py (added `security` pytest marker; removed unused `os` import discovered in session)
- Fixed pre-existing non-executable bit on support-mapping-dual-spring-kafka-001's 2 safe scripts (discovered by test_all_tasks_valid.py)

## Follow-ups filed

title: Run 12 cognitive ablation runs for Phase 1 pilot
description:
## Context

The Phase 1 PRD requires cognitive ablation on tasks 1 (incident-inv-docker-shutdown-004) and 3 (support-map-grafana-alerts-004) — the easiest and hardest conversions. Each task has 2 repos, so 2 ablation variants per task x 3 reps = 12 runs total. These are the last must-have runs before Phase 1 is complete.

## What to run

### Task 1: incident-inv-docker-shutdown-004 (moby + containerd)
- Ablation A: remove moby, keep containerd only (3 reps)
- Ablation B: remove containerd, keep moby only (3 reps)

### Task 3: support-map-grafana-alerts-004 (grafana + alertmanager)
- Ablation C: remove grafana, keep alertmanager only (3 reps)
- Ablation D: remove alertmanager, keep grafana only (3 reps)

Total: 12 runs. Budget: ~$25-60 (PRD allocates $100-240 for all 48, 36 already spent).

## How to run

The ablation script is ready:

```bash
# Task 1: incident-inv-docker-shutdown-004
scripts/validation/run_crnt_ablation.sh \
  benchmarks/incident_response/incident-investigation-004/ \
  --reps 3 --mode baseline

# Task 3: support-map-grafana-alerts-004
scripts/validation/run_crnt_ablation.sh \
  benchmarks/customer_escalation/support-mapping-004/ \
  --reps 3 --mode baseline
```

Each invocation ablates all repos in the task (2 per task), running 3 reps each. Runs in parallel across accounts 1-5.

## Output
- Results in `results/runs/{task_id}/ablate-{repo}/rep{N}/`
- Per-checkpoint scores in each `results.json`

## Acceptance criteria
- 12 results.json files exist across the 4 ablation variants
- Agent scores ≤ 30% on checkpoints anchored to the removed repo (PRD threshold)
- Results logged to run manifest

## After completion
- Run `python scripts/validation/verify_grounding.py <task_dir> --results-dir results/runs/<task_id>/` to validate grounding
- Update `results/phase1_pilot/run_manifest.json` with ablation entries

## Key files
- `scripts/validation/run_crnt_ablation.sh` — ablation runner
- `scripts/orchestration/run_task.py` — underlying task runner (--ablation-variant flag)
- `benchmarks/incident_response/incident-investigation-004/task.toml`
- `benchmarks/customer_escalation/support-mapping-004/task.toml`

## Prerequisites
- Docker daemon running
- Sufficient disk space (≥20GB for concurrent builds)
- API accounts 1-5 configured

(6) Both tasks pass CRNT validation.

## Phases (as evidenced by closed/open children)

### Phase 1 — Session-type validation [closed: 0rv.1]

Established the session-type taxonomy and validated the 12-task pilot
end-to-end.

### Phase 1.5 — Cognitive ablation pilot [closed: 0rv.12, 0rv.13, 0rv.14]

Ran 12 cognitive ablation runs; ported CSB LLM judge to `eb_verify`;
wired LLM judge into `runner.py` as Tier 2 verification.

### Phase 2 — MCP parity with CSB [closed: 0rv.10 + .10.1–.10.6]

Centralized `sg_indexing_list.json`; preamble extracted to
`agents/harnesses/claude/mcp/sourcegraph.py`; fixed needs-auth MCP
flakiness; task schema enriched with `mcp_suite`, `repo_set_id`,
`org_scale`; validation registry + pre-flight script; per-repo
metadata enrichment.

### Phase 2.5 — Task design polish [closed: 0rv.15 redesign incident-inv-004; in flight: 0rv.18 prefer less-famous codebases]

Avoid model-memorized solutions by routing investigation tasks at
lesser-known code paths.

### Phase 3 — Task expansion to ~100 tasks across suites [in flight]

- 0rv.16 (in_progress): expected_solution.json for 112 active tasks
- 0rv.17 (slung): verification_modes = llm_curator across multi-repo tasks
- Per-suite curation (children outside the 0rv.* numbering):
  - 4nh — feature_delivery (19 tasks)
  - 6dr — technical_debt (23 tasks)
  - auu — incident_response (14 tasks, blocked on 9xx path-drift fix)
  - hyu — customer_escalation (23 tasks)
  - jtc — dependency_management (43 tasks)
  - np7 — platform_engineering (15 tasks)
  - ski — security_operations (2 tasks)

### Phase 4 — Validate cognitive ablation re-run [in flight]

- 0rv.19: re-run 12 cognitive ablation runs with LLM judge to confirm
  Phase 1.5 results survive the new task-mix and verification stack.

### Phase 8 — Paper and packaging [open: 0rv.8]

The publishable artifact. Gated on Phases 3–4 closing cleanly.

## Active blockers

- 0rv.24 — Output path mismatch in 10 new tasks (prompts vs checks)
- 0rv.25 — Ground truth correctness bugs in 4 new tasks + boilerplate checks
- 0rv.23 (closed, kept for regression vigilance) — SECURITY: shell injection
  in 28 of 30 new task check scripts

## Acceptance

- ~100 tasks across the established suite mix, all with `expected_solution.json`
- Three tool-access modes (no-tools / sg-only / mcp), comparable across tasks
- Tier 2 LLM-judge verification wired and validated (Phase 4 cognitive
  ablation re-run reproduces Phase 1.5 results within tolerance)
- Reproducibility: dual independent runs match within tolerance
- Paper draft + packaging artifacts ship via 0rv.8

## Out of scope

- New session types beyond the established taxonomy (Phase 1)
- Suites beyond the seven listed in Phase 3
- Replacing sourcegraph as the default MCP backend (separate decision)

title: Fix copy-paste bugs in 5 Group B check scripts (wrong repo prefix)
description:
Discovered while fixing 0rv.24: 5 dual-repo tasks have check scripts that read from a /workspace/<repo>/ path where <repo> is NOT one of the task's actual repos — likely copy-paste from a sister task.

| Task | Repos | Check expects | Should be |
|---|---|---|---|
| schema-evolution-dual-django-001 | django + wagtail | /workspace/zulip/SCHEMA_IMPACT.md | /workspace/django/SCHEMA_IMPACT.md or repo-agnostic |
| schema-evolution-dual-sentry-001 | sentry + relay | /workspace/zulip/SCHEMA_IMPACT.md | /workspace/sentry/SCHEMA_IMPACT.md or repo-agnostic |
| config-drift-dual-argocd-001 | argo-cd + argo-helm | /workspace/charts/DRIFT_REPORT.json | acceptable (charts/ is a real subdir of argo-helm), but inconsistent with peers |
| config-drift-dual-prometheus-001 | prometheus + thanos | /workspace/charts/DRIFT_REPORT.json | /workspace/prometheus/DRIFT_REPORT.json or repo-agnostic |
| dead-code-dual-k8s-001 | kubernetes + client-go | /workspace/react/dead_code_report.json | /workspace/kubernetes/dead_code_report.json or repo-agnostic |

For 0rv.24, instructions were aligned to the (buggy) check paths so the path-equality test passes. The deeper fix (renaming check paths to coherent values) is this bead.

Suggest: standardize all multi-repo task outputs to /workspace/agent_output/answer.json (depends on bead C2 follow-up) — once that lands, these tasks just need their check scripts updated to read from agent_output/answer.json and the per-task path becomes irrelevant.

Acceptance:
- All 5 tasks have check scripts that read from a path naming a real repo of the task (or use the answer.json convention)
- Instructions still match check paths
- Path-equality regression test still passes
notes:
RESOLVED: Audit of the 5 listed tasks found 3 already met acceptance after 0rv.24 (django/sentry/k8s checks read from a path naming a real repo: django, sentry, kubernetes respectively). Only argocd and prometheus still had the /workspace/charts/... copy-paste bug.

Fix: aligned check REPORT path to the real-repo path that the instruction tells the agent to write to:
- config-drift-dual-argocd-001: /workspace/charts/DRIFT_REPORT.json -> /workspace/argo-cd/DRIFT_REPORT.json (3 files)
- config-drift-dual-prometheus-001: /workspace/charts/DRIFT_REPORT.json -> /workspace/prometheus/DRIFT_REPORT.json (3 files)

Verification:
- tests/test_task_output_path_consistency.py: 113 passed, 45 skipped, 0 failed (was 2 failed)
- Adjacent suites (test_instruction_mcp_preamble + test_run_task_results + test_agent_trace_capture + test_mcp_config): 180 passed total

Out of scope (deeper copy-paste content bugs left for future bead):
- argocd/prometheus check_config_valid.sh still references /workspace/charts/bitnami/consul for an OPTIONAL CORRECTED_SERVICE branch that gracefully returns score 1.0 when the file is missing — content mismatch (the checks expect a Bitnami Consul Helm chart edit, not an argo-cd or prometheus edit) but not a path-equality violation.
- django/sentry checks grep for Zulip-specific patterns (actions/user_groups, namedusergroup_deactivated, etc.) — content-of-checks bug, not path-of-checks. Affects scoring fidelity but not the bead's acceptance.

Files changed (6): 3 check scripts each in argocd and prometheus task dirs.

title: Strip default answer.json appendix from runner for tasks reading task-specific paths
description:
scripts/orchestration/run_task.py:362-379 unconditionally appends a 'Write to /workspace/agent_output/answer.json' block to instruction.md before sending to the agent. This collides with tasks whose check scripts read from a task-specific path (e.g., /workspace/zulip/SCHEMA_IMPACT.md, /workspace/charts/DRIFT_REPORT.json, /workspace/<repo>/dead_code_report.json, plus most legacy single-repo tasks: dep-traversal-001..012, refactor-orchestration-001..008, monorepo-boundary-001..010, etc).

For these tasks the agent sees two conflicting output directives and may write only to answer.json, scoring 0.0 because the check reads the task-specific path.

Acceptance:
- Runner detects whether checks read agent_output/answer.json (e.g., grep for 'agent_output/answer.json' in checks/*.sh)
- If task does NOT use answer.json convention, skip the appendix and trust the task's own instruction.md to specify the output path
- Existing answer.json-using tasks unchanged (~75% of suite)
- tests/test_task_output_path_consistency.py continues to pass

Discovered in plan-review for 0rv.24 as item C2.
notes:
RESOLVED: Added _task_uses_answer_json_convention(task_dir) helper in scripts/orchestration/run_task.py. _build_instruction_text now skips the default '/workspace/agent_output/answer.json' output appendix when no check script in the task's checks/ directory references that path. Tasks whose checks read task-specific paths (e.g. analysis/IMPACT_REPORT.md, charts/DRIFT_REPORT.json, prometheus/DRIFT_REPORT.json, plus all legacy single-repo tasks: dep-traversal-001..012, refactor-orchestration-001..008, monorepo-boundary-001..010, etc.) now trust their own instruction.md as the sole output directive. Backward-compat: tasks with no checks/ directory keep the default appendix. Affects ~113 of ~158 active tasks. Audit: comm against grep of 'agent_output/answer.json' across benchmarks/*/*/checks/*.sh confirmed 113 task dirs do not use the answer.json convention.

Tests added (TDD): TestConditionalOutputAppendix in tests/test_instruction_mcp_preamble.py — 6 cases covering appendix included/omitted across check-script variants, baseline + mcp_only modes, and the no-checks-dir backward-compat fallthrough. Existing 21 MCP preamble tests unchanged. tests/test_task_output_path_consistency.py: 138 passed, 45 skipped, 2 pre-existing failures (config-drift-dual-{argocd,prometheus}-001) tracked under EnterpriseBench-7k4 — unrelated, confirmed unchanged when stashed before/after this edit. Adjacent suites (test_run_task_results, test_agent_trace_capture, test_mcp_config): 67 passed with clean env.

Files changed (2): scripts/orchestration/run_task.py (+97/-24), tests/test_instruction_mcp_preamble.py (+108).

title: security: path-spam substring-match cleanup in cousin check scripts
description: Benchmarks/incident_response/ccx-incident-032/checks/check_source_files.sh, benchmarks/customer_escalation/support-mapping-0{01..04}/checks/check_code_paths.sh, and benchmarks/_other/calibration-0{01..04}/checks/check_code_paths.sh use (gt_f in af or af.endswith(gt_f)) substring-match even though they're not shell-injection-vulnerable (already read via env vars). This lets agents cheat by spamming paths that substring-match required files. Tighten to (af == gt_f or af.endswith('/' + gt_f)) — same fix applied in 0rv.23 for the injection-vulnerable cohort.
notes: DONE: patched 9 files to use anchored suffix match (af==gt OR af.endswith('/'+gt)). Files: incident_response/ccx-incident-032/checks/check_source_files.sh; customer_escalation/support-mapping-00{1,2,3,4}/checks/check_code_paths.sh; customer_escalation/calibration-00{5,6,7,8}/checks/check_code_paths.sh (note: bead description said _other/calibration-001..004 but actual location is customer_escalation/calibration-005..008). 3 patterns (inline lambda in ccx-032; path_match() with normalize in support-mapping; path_match() inline in calibration). Smoke test verified: bare basename, substring, and reverse-suffix patterns all rejected; legit suffix-with-slash and exact paths still pass.

title: BLOCKER: Ground truth correctness bugs in 4 new tasks + boilerplate checks
description:
Four new tasks from bead 0rv.20 have ground truth correctness bugs that will penalize correct agents or allow agents to pass without doing the work. Plus a systemic issue: 9 of 10 tasks have boilerplate checkpoint scripts that all measure the same thing.

Discovered during /focus parallel convergence review.

## Individual ground truth bugs

### 1. dead-code-dual-spring-hibernate-001: nonexistent hibernate5 path
Ground truth required_files references:
  spring-orm/src/main/java/org/springframework/orm/hibernate5/HibernateTransactionManager.java

But Spring Framework 6.0 (pinned at v6.0.11) dropped the hibernate5 ORM sub-package entirely — replaced by Hibernate 6 native Jakarta EE support. Path does not exist at pinned revision. An agent correctly finding this to be absent would be penalized.

Fix: Replace with a valid path from spring-framework v6.0.11, e.g.:
  spring-orm/src/main/java/org/springframework/orm/jpa/vendor/HibernateJpaVendorAdapter.java

### 2. schema-evolution-dual-spring-flyway-001: CRNT failure (one-repo solvable)
Both required_files are in spring-boot:
  - FlywayAutoConfiguration.java (spring-boot)
  - HibernateJpaAutoConfiguration.java (spring-boot)

The only spring-framework file is in sufficient_files. Agent that never opens spring-framework gets full score. Violates Cross-Repo Necessity Test — cannot be declared dual_repo.

Fix: Promote spring-context/src/main/java/org/springframework/context/support/AbstractApplicationContext.java from sufficient_files to required_files, OR add a spring-framework file genuinely required for the initialization ordering question (e.g., BeanFactoryPostProcessor interface location).

### 3. config-drift-dual-tokio-hyper-001: prompt references removed API
Prompt says: "trace through Http::new() and Server::bind()..."

At hyper v1.0.0-rc.4, the legacy high-level Server::bind() API was removed. hyper v1.0 uses hyper::server::conn::http1::Builder instead. Prompt will actively mislead agents.

Fix: Update prompt to use v1.x API terminology (hyper::server::conn::http1::Builder, service-based architecture).

### 4. dep-graph-dual-nextjs-swc-001: task ID/repos mismatch
- Task ID and repo_set_id say SWC
- Actual repos: vercel/next.js + webpack/webpack (no SWC repo)
- check_version_pinning.sh and check_transform_mapping.sh comments describe SWC analysis
- repo_set_id "nextjs-webpack-ecosystem" collides with support-mapping-dual-nextjs-webpack-001

Fix: Rename task to dep-graph-dual-nextjs-webpack-001. Update ID in task.toml, ground_truth.json, and the two check script comment lines. Rename directory.

### 5. refactor-dual-flask-werkzeug-001: false-precision sufficient file
sufficient_files includes src/werkzeug/serving.py (confidence 0.70), but this is the dev server, unrelated to the LocalStack/contextvars migration. The real implementation is in src/werkzeug/local.py (already required). Penalizes agents that correctly focus on local.py.

Fix: Remove src/werkzeug/serving.py from sufficient_files, or replace with src/werkzeug/sansio/utils.py.

## Systemic issue: boilerplate checks (9 of 10 tasks)

9 of 10 new tasks have 3 checkpoint scripts that all run identical file-path matching logic. Example: config-drift-dual-tokio-hyper-001 has check_config_valid.sh, check_drift_points.sh, check_expected_values.sh — all doing the same thing. The 0.20 / 0.45 / 0.35 weighting is meaningless.

Only support-mapping-dual-spring-kafka-001 has semantically distinct checks (error_chain keyword matching, trigger_conditions matching).

Fix options:
a) Differentiate each task's 3 checks: one for file discovery, one for content/keyword matching, one for structural validation (JSON schema, report completeness)
b) Consolidate to 1 check per task and rewrite task.toml to have a single checkpoint
c) Follow spring-kafka's pattern and add task-specific semantic fields to ground_truth.json that each check reads

## Affected tasks

Correctness bugs: 4 tasks (listed above) + supabase tri-task language tag fix already applied
Boilerplate checks: all 10 new tasks except spring-kafka

## Acceptance criteria

- All 5 individual ground truth bugs fixed (paths valid at pinned revs, CRNT passes, prompt references match pinned API)
- dep-graph-swc task renamed to dep-graph-webpack (directory + task.toml id + ground_truth.json + check comments)
- All 10 tasks have semantically distinct checkpoint logic OR consolidated to single checkpoint
- CRNT validation passes for all 10 tasks: python3 scripts/validation/crnt_validator.py <task-dir>
- No checkpoint script returns a grade based solely on another checkpoint's inputs

Depends on: 0rv.24 (output path fix) — some of these ground truth fixes touch the same files

Priority: P2 (tasks functional after 0rv.23/0rv.24 but produce unfair or uninformative scores until fixed)
notes: SCOPE ADDITION: Restoring 'go' tag on schema-evolution-tri-supabase-001 (done 2026-04-11 as part of review fix) moved Go back to 40.7% of multi-repo. Need 1 more non-Go multi-repo task to get back below 40% cap. Either add 1 new task or do one more defensible Go tag removal.

metadata.gc.note: Primary AC all met: 4 GT bugs fixed (1 was false premise — hibernate5 path verified to exist at v6.0.11), rename complete with all references updated, 9 of 10 tasks differentiated following spring-kafka pattern, CRNT PASS on all 10. Scope-addition (Go% 40.7% over 40% cap) escalated to mayor for direction (mail gc-65395).

title: Distinguish infra errors from agent failures in run_task.py
description:
run_task.py currently reports OOM, disk-full, and timeout as score=0.00 completed, making infrastructure failures indistinguishable from agent failures in results analysis.

Per PRD task_mix_realignment Must-Have requirement: OOM (exit 137), disk full, and timeout (exit 124) must produce infra_error status, not score=0.00 completed.

Acceptance criteria:
- run_task.py detects exit code 137 (OOM) and sets status=infra_error
- run_task.py detects exit code 124 (timeout) and sets status=infra_error
- run_task.py detects disk-full conditions and sets status=infra_error
- infra_error results are excluded from aggregate scoring in runner.py
- At least one test per failure mode
close_reason: ALREADY DONE: run_task.py already handles infra errors comprehensively — exit 137 (infra_oom), exit 124 (infra_timeout), disk-full (infra_disk), build failures (infra_build), clone failures (infra_clone), auth failures (infra_auth). failure_class is saved to results JSON. phase=agent_infra_error prevents overwrite to 'complete'. Minor gap: aggregate scoring scripts don't explicitly filter infra_error results, but that's a reporting enhancement, not the original PRD requirement.

title: Fix Go ecosystem concentration (45.9% → <40% of multi-repo)
description:
Go ecosystem is 45.9% of multi-repo tasks (86 total), exceeding the 40% PRD cap. Need ~12 non-Go multi-repo tasks to dilute below 40%, OR convert/retire some Go multi-repo tasks.

Preferred approach: author new multi-repo tasks in Python (Django/Wagtail, Flask/Werkzeug, Celery/Kombu), Java (Spring/Kafka, Jackson/Spring Boot), Rust (tokio/hyper/axum), and TypeScript (Next.js/SWC) ecosystems per PRD §Design Considerations.

Acceptance criteria:
- `python3 scripts/validation/task_mix_validator.py` shows no ecosystem > 40%
- All new tasks pass CRNT validation
- All new tasks use real OSS repos with actual dependency chains
- Strict multi-repo stays >= 45% after changes
notes: POST-CLOSE FOLLOW-UPS: Convergence review found critical issues in the 10 new tasks. Filed blockers: 0rv.23 (SECURITY shell injection in 28 scripts), 0rv.24 (output path mismatch — all tasks score 0.0 at runtime), 0rv.25 (4 ground truth bugs + boilerplate checks). Also: restoring 'go' tag on schema-evolution-tri-supabase-001 (required_files include gotrue .go paths) moved Go back to 40.7% of multi-repo — need 1 more non-Go multi-repo task or one more defensible tag cleanup to get below 40%. Config-drift-004 tag cleanup stands (go-template != Go language).

title: Power analysis for mode discrimination significance
description: The Phase 1 pilot ran n=12 tasks for mode discrimination (baseline vs mcp_only vs hybrid), which is insufficient for statistical significance at p<0.1. A proper power analysis is needed to determine the required sample size for detecting meaningful score differences between modes. The pilot showed effect sizes and variance that can inform the power calculation. This bead covers: (1) formal power analysis using pilot data (effect size, variance, desired alpha=0.1, power=0.8), (2) determine required n per mode, (3) design a stratified sampling plan that ensures sufficient tasks per suite/difficulty combination, (4) document the analysis so the paper (Phase 8) can reference the statistical methodology.
acceptance_criteria:
1. Power analysis script exists (e.g., scripts/analysis/power_analysis.py) that takes pilot data as input and outputs required sample size.
2. Analysis uses appropriate test (e.g., paired t-test or Wilcoxon signed-rank) matching the planned comparison methodology.
3. Parameters documented: effect size from pilot (Cohen's d or equivalent), observed variance, alpha=0.1, power=0.8.
4. Required n determined and documented. If n exceeds available tasks (112), document the achievable power at n=112 and recommend whether to expand the task set.
5. Stratified sampling plan: specifies how many tasks per suite and difficulty level are needed, accounting for the non-uniform distribution of tasks across suites.
6. Output: a written methodology section suitable for inclusion in the Phase 8 paper, covering power analysis, sample size justification, and stratification approach.
7. Pilot data (n=12 results) preserved as input fixture for reproducibility.
close_reason: Closed

title: Ecosystem diversification beyond K8s concentration
description: Kubernetes ecosystem repos (repo_set_id containing 'kubernetes') account for ~21 of 62 multi-repo tasks (34%), creating concentration risk: if K8s-specific patterns are overrepresented, benchmark scores may not generalize to other ecosystems. This bead tracks the effort to diversify the task portfolio. Target ecosystems for new tasks: (1) Rust/cargo ecosystem (e.g., tokio, hyper, tonic), (2) Python packaging ecosystem (e.g., pip, setuptools, poetry interactions), (3) Java/Maven ecosystem (e.g., Spring Boot, Hibernate cross-module), (4) CNCF non-K8s projects (e.g., Prometheus + Thanos, Envoy + Istio data plane). Goal: reduce K8s to <25% of multi-repo tasks by adding 15-20 tasks from other ecosystems. Coordinate with 0rv.18 (less famous codebases) for investigation task subset.
acceptance_criteria:
1. K8s ecosystem share of multi-repo tasks drops from 34% (21/62) to below 25%.
2. At least 3 new ecosystems represented with 4+ tasks each (e.g., Rust/tokio, Python/packaging, Java/Spring, CNCF-non-K8s).
3. New tasks pass CRNT validation and have expected_solution.json.
4. Task diversity report (scripts or query): shows repo_set_id distribution across all multi-repo tasks, confirms no single ecosystem exceeds 25%.
5. Each new ecosystem has at least one task in each difficulty tier (hard, expert) to ensure difficulty calibration is not ecosystem-dependent.
6. Coordinate with EnterpriseBench-0rv.18 to avoid duplicate effort on task design.

title: Historical breaking-change mining pipeline
description: Build a semi-automated end-to-end pipeline for mining historical breaking changes from OSS repos to generate new benchmark tasks. Currently, task authoring is manual (read changelogs, find PRs, trace code). This pipeline should: (1) scan CHANGELOG/release notes for breaking-change keywords, (2) extract linked PRs/commits, (3) identify cross-repo API surfaces affected, (4) generate candidate task.toml + instruction.md skeletons, (5) output candidates for human review and CRNT validation. Target repos: CNCF ecosystem, Go module ecosystem, Rust crate ecosystem. Pipeline should be runnable as a CLI script (e.g., scripts/mining/mine_breaking_changes.py) with configurable repo list and date range.
acceptance_criteria:
1. CLI script exists at scripts/mining/mine_breaking_changes.py (or similar) that accepts: repo URL, date range, output directory.
2. Pipeline stages implemented: (a) changelog/release-notes parser that extracts breaking-change entries, (b) PR/commit linker that resolves changelog entries to specific commits, (c) cross-repo impact analyzer that identifies downstream consumers affected, (d) skeleton generator that outputs draft task.toml + instruction.md for each candidate.
3. Output format: one directory per candidate task with draft task.toml, instruction.md, and metadata.json (source PR, confidence score, affected repos).
4. Tested on at least 3 repos from different ecosystems (e.g., gRPC-Go, containerd, etcd) producing at least 10 candidate tasks.
5. Human review step documented: reviewer checklist for promoting candidate to real task.
6. Integration with CRNT validator: candidates that pass automated CRNT checks are flagged as high-confidence.

title: Phase 2 task design: prefer less famous codebases for investigation tasks
description: Phase 1 showed Docker (moby) is too well-known for LLM judge to distinguish domain knowledge from code navigation. Phase 2 investigation tasks should prefer repos where: (1) function names are not in common training data, (2) internal structure is not widely documented, (3) the pinned revision has recent refactors not yet in training data. Candidates: less famous CNCF projects, internal enterprise tools, or niche but complex OSS repos.
acceptance_criteria:
1. Selection criteria for Phase 2 investigation tasks: (a) Repo function names must NOT appear in common LLM training data (avoid moby/daemon.go, well-known Docker entry points), (b) Internal structure must not be widely documented in tutorials/blog posts, (c) Prefer repos with recent refactors at the pinned revision that post-date common training data cutoffs, (d) Prefer cross-repo internal API interactions (e.g., containerd content store vs snapshotter) over well-known lifecycle hooks.
2. Candidate repos identified in 0rv.15 research: containerd content store + snapshot interactions, libnetwork sandbox cleanup races, image distribution/resolver internals, moby's daemon/containerd/ obscure files (image_commit.go, image_import.go, image_snapshot.go). Expand candidate list to include non-Docker CNCF projects and niche-but-complex OSS repos.
3. Target count: Design 10-15 new investigation tasks using less-famous codebases. Each task must pass CRNT validation (scripts/validation/crnt_validator.py) confirming the task is grounded in real code at pinned revisions.
4. CRNT validation requirement: Every new task must have (a) task.toml with all required fields, (b) instruction.md with clear prompt, (c) expected_solution.json with function-level criteria, (d) CRNT validator pass (all referenced files/functions exist at pinned commit). Tasks that fail CRNT validation must not be merged.
5. Kubernetes ecosystem concentration must decrease: current K8s repo_set_id tasks are ~21 of 62 multi-repo tasks (34%). New tasks should use non-K8s ecosystems to bring K8s below 30% of total multi-repo tasks.
6. Each new task PR must include the CRNT validation output in the PR description.
notes:
RESEARCH FINDINGS from 0rv.15 work: The incident-inv-004 redesign surfaced key learnings for Phase 2 task design. For investigation tasks, prefer repos where: (1) the bug involves cross-repo internal APIs not commonly discussed (e.g., containerd's pkg/unpack/unpacker.go is far less famous than daemon/monitor.go), (2) the interaction pattern involves subsystem boundaries (content store vs snapshotter) rather than well-known lifecycle hooks, (3) real bug PRs exist for ground truth but the code paths aren't in popular Docker tutorials/blog posts. Candidates identified: containerd content store + snapshot interactions, libnetwork sandbox cleanup races, image distribution/resolver internals. Also: moby's daemon/containerd/ package has many obscure files (image_commit.go, image_import.go, image_snapshot.go) that are less famous than daemon/daemon.go or daemon/monitor.go.

title: Add verification_modes = llm_curator to all multi-repo tasks
description:
Once expected_solution.json exists for a task, enable llm_curator in its task.toml verification_modes. Single-repo calibration tasks can stay deterministic-only. Multi-repo tasks are the priority since they have richer ground truth that benefits from LLM evaluation.

Blocked by: 0rv.16 (expected_solution.json creation)

Acceptance criteria:
- All multi-repo tasks with expected_solution.json have verification_modes including llm_curator in task.toml
- Calibration tasks retain deterministic-only verification (no llm_curator required)
- grep -rL 'llm_curator' benchmarks/*/task.toml returns only calibration and single-repo tasks without expected_solution.json
- No task has llm_curator enabled without a corresponding expected_solution.json
acceptance_criteria:
1. All multi-repo tasks (difficulty_stratum in [dual_repo, tri_repo, multi_repo, quad_repo], currently ~62 tasks) must have verification_modes = ["deterministic", "llm_curator"] in their task.toml.
2. Single-repo calibration tasks (difficulty_stratum = large_single or similar) retain verification_modes = ["deterministic"] only — no llm_curator added.

title: Phase 1 pilot investigation: baseline outperforms MCP on primary-repo-heavy tasks
description:
## Context

Phase 1 pilot ran 36 full runs (4 tasks x 3 modes x 3 reps). Mode discrimination gate passes (2/4 tasks with Cohen's d > 0.5), but 2/4 tasks show NEGATIVE discrimination — baseline outperforms hybrid/MCP.

## Findings (already investigated, needs writeup)

### incident-inv-docker-shutdown-004 (d = -0.82)
- Discriminating checkpoint: `remediation` (weight=0.20, deps moby-only)
- baseline avg: 0.67, mcp_only avg: 0.17, hybrid avg: 0.50
- Root cause: moby-only checkpoint. MCP agent spent 29 SG calls but generated only 11K tokens vs baseline's 23K — SG search breadth displaced depth
- All other checkpoints (~0.95+) identical across modes

### support-map-grafana-alerts-004 (d = -1.18)  
- Discriminating checkpoints: `code_paths` (w=0.60, grafana-only) + `ownership` (w=0.15)
- mcp_only: 111 SG calls, 17K tokens. baseline: 0 SG, 23K tokens
- Root cause: 60% of score on grafana-only checkpoint. SG search noisier than local grep for known-structure codebases

### Structural pattern (3 causes)
1. **Breadth-depth tradeoff**: MCP agents produce 20-30% fewer output tokens
2. **Primary-repo-heavy tasks**: 55-75% score weight on single-repo checkpoints → MCP adds noise not signal
3. **Search vs navigation**: local grep beats SG keyword_search for well-signposted paths. MCP shines when agent doesn't know where to look (config-drift-004 baseline scored 0.00 on rep1)

### Resolution
- Removed `repo_deps` from all tasks (was overengineering)
- Simplified CRNT to structural check (required_files in each repo)
- Phase 2 design implication: tasks need cross-repo *investigation paths*, not just cross-repo file references

## Deliverable
Write `docs/phase1_pilot_analysis.md` covering:
1. Per-task per-mode score tables
2. The 3 structural causes of negative discrimination
3. Design guidelines for Phase 2 task authoring (cross-repo investigation paths)
4. Gate verdict and statistical summary

## Key files
- `results/phase1_pilot/summary_scored.csv` — actual scores
- `scripts/analysis/mode_discrimination_gate.py` — gate script
- `results/runs/{task_id}/{mode}/rep{N}/` — per-run results with traces
notes:
PLAN: Write docs/phase1_pilot_analysis.md with 4 sections: (1) per-task per-mode score tables with checkpoint breakdowns, (2) 3 structural causes of negative discrimination (breadth-depth tradeoff, primary-repo-heavy tasks, search vs navigation), (3) Phase 2 design guidelines, (4) gate verdict and statistical summary. All data gathered from CSV and per-rep results.json.
REVIEW: Fixed 2 HIGH issues (Cause 1 token claim overgeneralized, incident-inv token range misleading), 3 MEDIUM issues (related_issues cross-ref, small-sample caveat, imprecise task count). 0 CRITICAL.
close_reason: DONE: Wrote docs/phase1_pilot_analysis.md with 4 sections: (1) per-task per-mode score tables with checkpoint breakdowns for all 4 pilot tasks, (2) 3 structural causes of negative discrimination (breadth-depth tradeoff, primary-repo-heavy design, search vs navigation), (3) 5 Phase 2 design guidelines (cross-repo investigation paths, 40% cross-repo weight, discovery difficulty calibration, saturation avoidance, related_issues validation), (4) gate verdict (PASS, 2/4 tasks with d>0.5) with token/cost summary and small-sample caveat. Review addressed 2 HIGH issues in Cause 1 token claims.

title: Add runtime token watchdog to batch runner
description: CSB harness_runner.py has background watchdog thread: monitors last 10 results, if >50% are invalid_output AND tokens expired, kills harness with SIGTERM (exit 2). Prevents burning compute on expired tokens. Port to run_benchmark.py parallel execution path.
notes:
PLAN: Add token watchdog to run_benchmark.py. Goal: prevent runaway costs by monitoring cumulative cost_usd across tasks. Approach: (1) Add --budget-usd CLI flag (default: no limit). (2) After each task completes, read its results.json tool_usage.cost_usd and accumulate. (3) At configurable warn threshold (default 80%), log a warning. (4) At 100% budget, stop submitting new tasks and exit with code 3. (5) Works in both sequential and parallel execution paths. Files: scripts/run_benchmark.py (add TokenBudget dataclass, budget tracking, CLI flags), tests/test_run_benchmark.py (add watchdog tests). Tests: budget exceeded stops execution, warning threshold triggers log, no budget means unlimited, partial results still written. Risks: in parallel mode tasks already in flight continue past budget. Out of scope: per-task token limits, real-time streaming token monitoring within a running task.
PROGRESS: TDD complete. 32/32 tests pass. Added TokenBudget dataclass, extract_task_cost function, --budget-usd/--budget-warn-pct CLI flags, budget tracking in sequential and parallel execution paths, cost data in summary.json, exit code 3 on budget exceeded.
REVIEW: Code reviewed. No CRITICAL/HIGH issues. TokenBudget is immutable, extract_task_cost is defensive, sequential path stops before next task, parallel path stops between batches. Backward compatible -- no behavior change without --budget-usd flag. Exit code 3 for budget stop.
close_reason: DONE: Added runtime token watchdog to batch runner. TokenBudget frozen dataclass tracks cumulative cost_usd. --budget-usd sets max spend, --budget-warn-pct sets warning threshold (default 80%). Sequential path checks budget before each task; parallel path checks after each completion. Summary includes cumulative_cost_usd and budget_usd. Exit code 3 on budget exceeded. 20 new tests (32 total), all passing.

title: Phase 7: Analysis and reporting
description: Score distributions per suite/difficulty/mode, MCP benefit delta calculations, calibration bias check, reproducibility stats, visualization pipeline. Done when: complete analysis report with charts and statistical tests.
close_reason: DONE: Phase 7 complete. 3 scripts: analyze_scores.py (73 results loaded, distributions/deltas/calibration/stats), generate_charts.py (8 charts), generate_report.py (comprehensive markdown report). 86 total tests. Pipeline: analyze_scores → generate_charts → generate_report.

title: Phase 8: Paper and packaging
description: Benchmark paper (methodology, results, analysis), external release packaging, documentation and setup guide. Done when: paper draft complete, release artifacts packaged.
acceptance_criteria:
1. Paper outline document exists with sections: Abstract, Introduction (motivation + gap), Benchmark Design (task taxonomy, repo selection, difficulty stratification, CRNT grounding), Evaluation Framework (session types, verification modes, scoring), Experimental Setup (agent configs, MCP modes, cost tracking), Results (score distributions by suite/difficulty/mode, MCP benefit deltas, reproducibility stats), Discussion (limitations, threats to validity), Related Work, Conclusion.
2. Required figures/tables defined and generated: (a) Table: task taxonomy (suite x difficulty x session_type counts), (b) Figure: score distribution violin plots per suite, (c) Figure: MCP benefit delta bar chart (baseline vs mcp_only vs hybrid), (d) Table: reproducibility stats (mean, std, ICC for 3x runs on 20-30 task subset), (e) Figure: cost per task scatter plot, (f) Table: CRNT grounding coverage summary.
3. Release checklist completed: (a) All 112+ task definitions pass bd verify dry-run, (b) README with setup instructions (clone, install deps, configure agent, run single task, run full sweep), (c) LICENSE file present, (d) configs/ and benchmarks/ directories contain no secrets or internal paths, (e) scripts/eb_verify and scoring pipeline runnable from clean checkout, (f) expected_solution.json present for all tasks requiring llm_curator mode, (g) Sample output included for 1 task to demonstrate expected results format.
4. Concrete deliverables: (a) paper.tex or paper.md draft with all sections filled, (b) figures/ directory with generated plots, (c) RELEASE.md with packaging instructions, (d) Makefile or script target for reproducible figure generation from raw results.
5. Depends on: Phase 7 analysis output (score distributions, reproducibility report, MCP comparison data).

title: Phase 6: Complete sweep + reproducibility
description: Run remaining ~45 tasks in 3 modes, reproducibility validation (3x runs on 20-30 task subset, variance < 0.15), cost tracking aggregation. Done when: 100 tasks x 3 modes scored, reproducibility report generated.
notes: CONTEXT: As of 2026-04-01, 89 tasks scored in baseline mode (results/runs/), ~24 in MCP modes (results/mcp_batch*/). 111 total tasks available. Run infra: scripts/run_phase4.sh --account 1-5. MCP config via docker-cp (.mcp.json). Modes: baseline, mcp_only, hybrid. Reproducibility target: 3x runs on 20-30 tasks, variance < 0.15. Cost tracking needed from agent_trace.jsonl files.
close_reason: DONE: All 3 child scripts created (reproducibility_check.py, cost_tracker.py, run_sweep.py), 90 tests passing. Sweep manifest shows 182/333 scored, 150 pending, 1 failed. Coverage: baseline 60/111, mcp_only 61/111, hybrid 61/111. Remaining sweep execution is operational (human runs run_benchmark.py with --account 1-5).
